messages.content: What is another way of saying "offering offers to one's ancestors"?

Translator to English

"最近話題の某ゲーム… 北風の日を待っていたものの、光線無しでした。"
"The recently popular game... Although I was waiting for a day of northern winds, there was no ray of light."

Translate to English

"जपाकुसुम संकाशं काश्यपेयं महदद्युतिम् I
तमोरिंसर्वपापघ्नं प्रणतोSस्मि दिवाकरम् II १ II"
"I pray to the Sun, the day-maker, destroyer of all sins, the enemy of darkness, the splendid illuminator of the three worlds, who is like the flower of jasmine, the glory of the house of Kashyapa."

Identify this text

"“जपाकुसुम संकाशं काश्यपेयं महदद्युतिम् I
तमोरिंसर्वपापघ्नं प्रणतोSस्मि दिवाकरम् II १ II”"
This is a verse from the Hindu scripture called the "Gayatri Mantra". It is a highly revered mantra in Hinduism and is dedicated to Lord Savitr, the Sun God.

Translate to Korean

"I think I filled my daily quota of reminding everyone of who much I hate Squid Game."
"나는 스쿼리드 게임을 얼마나 싫어하는지 다시 한번 상기시키는 일일 할당량을 채운 것 같다고 생각해요."

Translate to Spanish

“I think I filled my daily quota of reminding everyone of who much I hate Squid Game.”
"Creo que he cumplido con mi cuota diaria de recordar a todos cuánto odio el Juego del Calamar."

Translate to Spanish

"A number of porcupines huddled together for warmth on a cold day in winter, but, as they began to prick one another with their quills, they were obliged to disperse. However, the cold drove them together again, when just the same thing happened. At last, after many turns of huddling and dispersing, they discovered that they would be best off by remaining at a little distance from one another. In the same way, the need of society drives the human porcupines together, only to be mutually repelled by the many prickly and disagreeable qualities of their nature. The moderate distance which they at last discover to be the only tolerable condition of intercourse is the code of politeness and fine manners, and those who transgress it are roughly told—in the English phrase—to keep their distance. By this arrangement, the mutual need of warmth is only very moderately satisfied, but then people do not get pricked. A man who has some heat in himself prefers to remain outside, where he will neither prick other people nor get pricked himself."

Un grupo de puercoespines se apiñó para mantenerse calentito en un día frío de invierno, pero al comenzar a pincharse unos a otros con sus púas, tuvieron que dispersarse. Sin embargo, el frío los volvió a juntar, y justo lo mismo volvió a ocurrir. Tras muchos episodios de juntarse y dispersarse, finalmente descubrieron que lo mejor era mantenerse a una cierta distancia entre ellos. Del mismo modo, la necesidad de la sociedad hace que los puercoespines humanos se junten, solo para repelerse mutuamente debido a las muchas cualidades punzantes y desagradables de su naturaleza. La distancia moderada que finalmente descubren como la única condición tolerable para el trato humano es el código de la educación y los buenos modales, y a aquellos que lo incumplen se les dice bruscamente, con la frase inglesa, que mantengan su distancia. Se satisface modestamente la necesidad mutua de calor, pero entonces la gente no se pincha. Un hombre que tiene un poco de calor en sí mismo prefiere mantenerse afuera, donde no pinchará a los demás ni se pinchará a sí mismo.

Identify this quote

"The grace of our God be with you all. Amen."

This is a quote from the Bible, specifically the New Testament of the Christian Bible. It can be found in various books including Romans, 1 Corinthians, Galatians, Ephesians, Philippians, Colossians, 1 Thessalonians, 2 Thessalonians, 1 Timothy, Titus, Philemon, and Revelation. This quote is typically used as a benediction or closing prayer in Christian religious services.
What is the last verse in the Qur'an?
The last verse (ayah) in the Qur'an is Surah Al-Maidah, verse 3: "This day have I perfected your religion for you, completed My favour upon you, and have chosen for you Islam as your religion."
